headline: Chips Crater as Healthcare Rallies; Geopolitical Calm Steadies Oil Ahead of a Busy Week
body_html:
Friday's session closed on a broadly positive note — 109 names advanced against 89 decliners across the 198-stock universe — yet the headline numbers masked a dramatic fault line between sectors. Healthcare surged 3.17% and Consumer added 1.91%, while the semiconductor complex collapsed 6.5%, dragging the broader tape and producing some of the sharpest single-session losses of the year. Financial-data names led gainers, with Equifax climbing 6.1%, S&P Global adding 6.01%, and Fair Isaac rising 5.32%, suggesting investors rotated hard into data-and-analytics franchises perceived as insulated from chip-cycle volatility.

The semiconductor wreckage was broad and deep. Sandisk shed 14.1%, Teradyne fell 13.6%, Amkor Technology dropped 12.9%, KLA Corp lost 11.5%, and Entegris declined 11.2% — a sweep that signals either a demand-revision scare or supply-chain anxiety rather than any single company-specific event. Corning, exposed through its materials business, fell 10.8% in sympathy. Intuitive Surgical's 5.87% gain and Roche's 5.41% advance reinforced the view that capital was actively seeking shelter in healthcare franchises with visible near-term earnings.

Geopolitics will frame the macro backdrop entering the new week. Persian Gulf oil flows are reported near pre-war levels following U.S.-Iran diplomacy, keeping crude prices broadly stable — a relief for consumer-facing names after weeks of energy uncertainty. Investors will also monitor whether the semiconductor selloff deepens or stabilizes, and whether the financial-data cohort can sustain Friday's outsized momentum. Homebuilders (+1.03%) and Software (+0.74%) held constructive, while Industrials and Materials finished in the red, setting an uneven foundation for the sessions ahead.

briefs.headline: Financial-Data Trio Dominates the Leaderboard
briefs.body: Equifax, S&P Global, and Fair Isaac each gained more than 5% on Friday, collectively making Financial-Data the standout pocket of strength. The simultaneous surge across all three suggests a thematic rotation rather than isolated news, with investors possibly repricing the earnings durability of data-licensing business models relative to hardware-dependent peers.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Slide Raises Cycle-Turn Alarm
briefs.body: Five chip-related names — spanning memory, test equipment, packaging, process tools, and specialty materials — each fell between 11% and 14% in a single session. The breadth of the decline across distinct semiconductor sub-industries points to a sector-wide reassessment of near-term demand, and will likely dominate analyst commentary as the week opens.

briefs.headline: Persian Gulf Flows Near Pre-War Levels as Oil Holds Steady
briefs.body: Crude prices ended little changed on Friday as supply from the Persian Gulf approached levels seen before recent hostilities, reflecting the fragile but intact U.S.-Iran diplomatic track. Should flows remain uninterrupted, energy-cost pressures on industrial and consumer businesses may ease further in the weeks ahead.

briefs.headline: Iran Opens Oil Talks with Japanese Buyers Amid Sanctions Uncertainty
briefs.body: Tehran is reportedly exploring crude sales to Japanese purchasers while buyers seek extended sanctions waivers, according to sources familiar with the matter. The development adds a new dimension to the post-conflict energy landscape, potentially reshaping Asian supply relationships if diplomatic arrangements hold and formal exemptions are granted.

briefs.headline: Airbus Chief Strikes Cautious Tone on Pan-European Defence Industry
briefs.body: The chief executive of Airbus expressed measured pessimism about the pace of cooperation among European nations on defence industrial planning. The comments come as the continent debates how to accelerate production capacity, an issue that carries direct implications for aerospace-supply-chain stocks across the Industrials sector.

briefs.headline: World Cup Prediction Markets Reach Record Trading Volumes
briefs.body: Speculative activity on tournament outcome markets hit all-time highs this week as the football World Cup advances into its decisive rounds. While not a direct equity catalyst, the surge in event-based trading underscores elevated retail participation in risk markets — a sentiment gauge watched by behavioural-finance desks heading into the week.
